visualGoal: Make a budget builder confident in ONE balanced sub-$1,000 direct-drive cart (base + load-cell pedals + rigid mount) before the torque-trap warnings hit. Real product proof for every embedded pick; no procedural art.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The ~$199 folding mount that keeps the winning cart under $1,000. Currently TEXT-ONLY because no verified image and no product record. Needs a real photo (and a products/racing record) before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid. Body currently anchors the visible mount pick on the verified GT Omega Apex (the stretch option).
$199.99 tighter-budget load-cell alternative to the CRP2. Text-only in body until a real photo is sourced.
~$149-199 budget load-cell alt (100kg brake). Text-only. Note moza-srp-lite.jpg exists as a product record but is a different (Lite) SKU and is NOT verified.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic Gen X garage sim-racing bench at golden-hour rim light, a rigid folding wheel stand with a 4-bolt mounting plate left open for a real wheelbase product overlay and an empty pedal tray below, a price tag or budget slip reading a rounded total, curator Duke Alvarez laying out three boxes in priority order, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake wheelbase, fake pedals, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Where every dollar of $1,000 goes
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center stacked-budget chart, large readable text, racing colors, real product photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Budget build buy order
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, gesturing over a folding wheel stand holding a direct-drive base with load-cell pedals bolted below, confident mechanic energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Source a real NLR Wheel Stand 2.0 photo + add a products/racing record so the ~$199 mount that defines the winning cart can be a visible pick, not text-only?
